item_name: AI Vector / SVG Generation (Recraft, Vectorizer.ai, Magic Vector)
definition_origin: AI vector generation refers to tools that produce native SVG (true vector paths) or convert raster output into vectors via AI-assisted tracing. Recraft V3/V4 was the first model trained to natively output vector code (paths, fills, strokes) rather than rasterizing first; auxiliary tools like Vectorizer.ai, Vector Magic, SVG.io, SVGMaker.io and Inkscape's Trace Bitmap convert raster (PNG/JPG) into SVG. The category exists because designers historically had to draw every icon and logo by hand in Illustrator/Figma; AI now drafts the structural skeleton in seconds, leaving designers to clean up and brand-tune.
core_purpose: Vector assets carry visceral and behavioral weight in apps. At the visceral level (Norman) they are the first impression of polish: icons, logos, mascots, empty-state illustrations. At the behavioral level they communicate function reliably across resolutions (sharp at 1x, 2x, 3x, retina, web) without bloating bundles. AI vector generation lets a solo dev with zero design skill produce production-grade SVG tokens, app icons, onboarding spot-illustrations, and feature glyphs in minutes rather than days. For a broke solo dev the strategic value is that 1 tool replaces the 'hire a designer' line item entirely for icon-tier work.
when_needed: Consumer mobile: app icon, tab-bar icons, onboarding illustrations, empty-states, achievements/badges, sticker packs. B2B/dev-tools: feature glyphs, dashboard empty-states, brand logo, integration tile icons, marketing site spots. AI-wrapper apps: model/feature glyph icons, conversational UI decorations, hero spot illustration. Marketing surfaces (landing page, app store): hero illustrations, feature-row glyphs. Generally not needed for: photographic content, complex product photography, video.
format_options: SVG is the primary output. Pros: infinitely scalable, tiny file size (1-20KB typical for an icon), CSS-stylable (fill/stroke), animatable, accessible via <title>/<desc>. Cons: complex SVGs blow up in DOM nodes, can lag with hundreds of paths, and AI-generated SVGs often carry 5-10x the anchor points needed. PNG@1x/2x/3x: still required as fallback for legacy Android, App Store icon (1024x1024 PNG mandatory), splash screens. WebP: better compression than PNG for raster fallback (~30% smaller). PDF: vector format Apple sometimes prefers for asset catalogs (Xcode supports PDF assets). Lottie/Rive: when a vector asset must animate (loading spinner, micro-interaction). Recraft outputs SVG natively; Vectorizer.ai and Inkscape Trace Bitmap convert raster to SVG; SVG.io and SVGMaker.io output SVG but quality is mixed (verify by opening in text editor — real SVG has <path d='M...'>, fake SVGs embed base64 raster).
file_size_performance: Target <10KB per icon SVG, <50KB per illustration SVG. Run SVGO or svgo-loader on every asset (strips metadata, rounds coordinates, merges paths). Cap path count at <50 for icons, <200 for illustrations. For mobile, prefer pre-rendered PNGs if SVG has >300 paths (rasterizer overhead). For web, inline critical SVGs in HTML; lazy-load decorative ones.
free_tools: Recraft (free 50 credits/day, public-only outputs, native-SVG generation, V3/V4 model — best free vector AI in 2026); Vectorizer.ai (no real free tier as of 2026 — limited preview only, paid for downloads ~$10/mo or $0.20/conversion; Vector Magic similar pricing); SVG.io (limited free generations, often produces fake-SVG with embedded raster — verify); SVGMaker.io (free tier with watermark or limit, native SVG output); Inkscape (100% free, desktop, Trace Bitmap with Potrace engine — best free raster-to-vector for high-contrast logos, manual but reliable); Recraft's built-in 'Vectorize' tool (free with daily credits, AI-trace any raster image to SVG); Photopea (browser-based, free, can open PSD and export SVG paths); Figma free tier itself (free, manual icon drawing with Pen tool — slow but the gold standard cleanup destination).
paid_tools: Recraft Pro ($12/mo or $10/mo annual — 1000 credits, private generation, commercial license, brand-style locking — single best paid choice for solo dev needing vector mascot consistency); Vectorizer.ai subscription (~$9.99/mo for 100 credits — only worth it if doing high-volume raster-to-vector daily); Adobe Illustrator + Firefly Vector Model 2 ($22.99/mo Illustrator only, or $59.99/mo Creative Cloud — IP indemnification for commercial work, Text-to-Vector in Illustrator since 2025); Vector Magic ($7.95/mo or $295 desktop one-time — strong raster-to-vector but Vectorizer.ai generally beats it now). Tier guide: Starter $10-12/mo (Recraft Pro), Pro $20-30/mo (Illustrator+Firefly), Enterprise $50+ (full Adobe CC for IP indemnification).
gemini_pro_workflow: Gemini Pro does NOT natively output SVG. Workflow: (1) use Imagen 4 via Gemini Pro / Whisk to generate a high-contrast raster reference (icon style: 'flat 2D vector icon, clean geometric shapes, single color #5B6CFF on transparent, minimalist line icon, no gradients, no 3D, no shadows, isolated on white background, 512x512'); (2) save PNG; (3) feed PNG into Recraft's Vectorize tool (free) OR Vectorizer.ai OR Inkscape Trace Bitmap; (4) clean anchor points in Figma or Inkscape; (5) export optimized SVG. Critical Gemini Pro pattern: use it as a moodboard and ideation engine — generate 6-12 raster variations, pick the strongest 1-2 directions, then redraw/vectorize the chosen direction. Whisk shuts down April 30, 2026 — pivot to Gemini app + Imagen 4. Use Gemini conversational editing to iterate ('same style but rounder corners', 'remove the gradient'). For text-on-vector use Recraft directly because Imagen 4 still misspells.
workflow_steps: Step 1 (5 min): Define one-sentence brief with archetype tag — 'Caregiver app, soft gentle line icon, single accent color, friendly rounded corners'. Step 2 (10 min): Generate 6-8 raster references in Imagen 4 / Gemini Pro using the prompt template in ai_prompt_templates. Step 3 (3 min): Pick 1 direction; if it has an obvious vector look, jump to Recraft for native SVG. Otherwise feed the chosen raster into Recraft Vectorize or Vectorizer.ai. Step 4 (5 min): Open SVG in Inkscape — Path > Simplify (Ctrl+L) to reduce anchor count, Object > Align to grid for icon precision. Step 5 (10 min): Drop into Figma; create as a Component with size variants 16/20/24/32; ensure single-color fill bound to a design token. Step 6 (5 min): Run through SVGO (svgomg.net browser tool, free); confirm output <10KB. Step 7 (3 min): Export PNG@1x/2x/3x fallbacks for Android assets. Step 8 (5 min): Write alt text, drop into codebase (assets/ or app/icons/). Total: ~45 min/icon for a solo dev with zero design skill.
ai_prompt_templates: Template 1 (icon): 'Flat 2D vector icon of {object}, single color {hex}, minimalist line style, geometric, clean shapes, no gradients, no shadows, no 3D, transparent background, centered, 512x512, suitable for mobile app navigation'. Template 2 (mascot/character spot): 'Flat vector illustration of {character}, {archetype: friendly/professional/playful} expression, simple shapes, 2-3 color flat design, no gradients, no fine details, isolated on transparent background'. Template 3 (logo via Recraft): 'Logo for {brand_name}, {style: wordmark/lettermark/symbol}, {personality}, native SVG, single color, no text effects, no gradient'. Template 4 (empty-state illustration): 'Spot illustration: {scene}, flat 2D vector style, soft palette ({color1}, {color2}, {color3}), friendly characters, light optimistic mood, 800x600, no realistic textures, no AI 3D look'. Counter-slop addendum to ALL prompts: 'no isometric voxel, no melted faces, no plastic skin, no purple-blue gradient, no generic chibi, no default Stable Diffusion aesthetic'.
claude_code_integration: Claude Code can: (1) generate React Native components wrapping SVGs via react-native-svg ('install react-native-svg, convert this SVG to a typed RN component with color prop'); (2) batch-optimize asset folders (write a script that runs svgo on assets/icons/*.svg with target plugins); (3) create asset registries (auto-generate src/icons/index.ts mapping name -> component); (4) write Expo SDK asset configs; (5) generate matching @1x/@2x/@3x PNG fallbacks via sharp; (6) lint for SVG bloat (CI step that fails on SVG >20KB or path-count >100). For web, Claude Code can wire SVGs as React components via @svgr/webpack or vite-plugin-svgr. For React Native specifically, react-native-svg-transformer enables direct SVG imports. Claude Code can also auto-generate Storybook stories for the icon component to preview every icon at every size.
emotional_design_integration: Vector style itself signals archetype. Caregiver: rounded corners (8-16dp radius), single soft warm color (#F4A261 / #E76F51), gentle line weight (2px stroke), open shapes — feels safe. Magician: thin 1px lines, deep purple/black, geometric precision, hint of asymmetry — feels mysterious. Jester: bold 3px outlines, bright primary palette, exaggerated proportions, rounded ends — feels playful. Hero: angular geometry, strong contrast, bold colors, sharp corners — feels powerful. Sage: monoline, monochrome, classical proportions — feels authoritative. The same 'home' icon can read as Caregiver (rounded house with heart) or Hero (angular shield-shape). At Norman's Visceral level the vector style is the first 50ms judgment — clean SVGs feel 'made-with-care', AI-slop SVGs feel 'spammy'. At Behavioral level, consistent line-weight and corner-radius across icon-sets produces predictable affordance.
specific_examples: Linear (linear.app) — every icon hand-drawn, monoline 1.5px, geometric, signals Sage/Magician archetype precision. Stripe (stripe.com) — vibrant gradients on flat shapes, custom illustrations for each product page, signals Hero (powerful but approachable). Duolingo (duolingo.com) — flat 2D vector mascot Duo the owl in dozens of expression variants — pure Jester archetype, all SVG/Lottie. Notion (notion.so) — minimal monochrome line icons, generous whitespace, Sage archetype. Headspace (headspace.com) — friendly hand-drawn vector characters, warm palette, Caregiver archetype. Streak/Superhuman — opinionated icon kit with consistent 24px grid. Reference for inspiration: dribbble.com/tags/icon-set, iconjar archive, thenounproject.com (free icons with attribution under CC).
anti_ai_slop_traps: AI vector slop tells: (1) excessive anchor points (>200 per icon — looks fine on screen but 'feels off' when zoomed); (2) inconsistent line-weight within a single icon (AI mixes 1px and 3px in same path); (3) generic blue-to-purple gradient on every shape (the 'purple plague'); (4) unintended decorative noise (random 'sparkle' particles or stray dots — AI loves to add 'magic'); (5) center-of-icon misalignment (AI rarely lands on a clean grid); (6) text-inside-icon misspelled or jumbled letterforms; (7) symmetrical-but-not-quite shapes (left and right halves don't mirror exactly); (8) drop shadows on supposedly 'flat' icons. Counter-strategies in prompt: 'no gradient, single solid color, no decorative particles, no shadows, on a 24-pixel grid, mirror-symmetric, no text inside'. Cleanup pass mandatory: open every AI SVG in Inkscape, run Path > Simplify (threshold 0.0002), then visually scan for: stray nodes, off-grid coordinates, hidden duplicate paths. If the icon doesn't look right at 16px (mobile tab-bar size), it's slop — redraw the silhouette manually with the Pen tool.
common_pitfalls: Performance: dropping a 200KB SVG into a list-row repeats the parse cost on every render — Inline only when truly small. Accessibility: forgetting <title> and aria-label on SVGs (screen readers announce nothing); never use SVG-text as the only label on a button. Consistency: mixing icon sets (Phosphor + Heroicons + Material in one app) — pick ONE family and stick. Color hardcoding: hardcoding #FF0000 in SVG fill instead of using fill='currentColor' for theme-able icons. Stroke-width: shipping outline icons that are visually too thin on mobile high-DPI (ship at 2px not 1px). Scope creep: spending 4 hours perfecting a single mascot when a 30-min icon would do. Bundle bloat: importing entire icon library when you use 12 icons — tree-shake or copy-paste only what you use.
accessibility_notes: All decorative SVGs: aria-hidden='true'. Functional SVG buttons: aria-label='close', or wrap with role='img' and <title>. Don't rely on color alone to signal state — pair with shape change. Provide reduced-motion variants for any animated SVG (Lottie/Rive). Ensure 3:1 contrast ratio between icon and background per WCAG 2.2 AA.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: ship SVG via react-native-svg (parses XML at runtime, slight cost) OR pre-rasterize @1x/2x/3x PNGs. App icon MUST be 1024x1024 PNG (Apple) and adaptive layered icon (Android) — vectors aren't accepted as the app launcher icon. Web: prefer inline SVG (CSS-stylable, smallest payload), or , or SVG sprite sheet. Web supports CSS animations on SVG; React Native needs Reanimated + react-native-svg-animations. Lottie works on both but file sizes differ: Lottie JSON often 5-50KB on web vs need to bundle Lottie runtime (~120KB) on RN.
sources: [Recraft Review 2026 — AIToolKits](https://aitoolkits.co/recraft-review-2026/), [Recraft V3 Revolution — Kingy AI](https://kingy.ai/ai/recraft-v3-revolutionizing-ai-image-and-vector-generation/), [Vectorizer.ai vs Inkscape vs Illustrator](https://www.yeschat.ai/blog-VectorizerAI-vs-Inkscape-vs-Illustrator-Which-is-Superior-35608), [10 Image-to-Vector Converters Tested 2026](https://vectosolve.com/blog/best-image-to-vector-converter-2026), [Best AI SVG Generators 2026](https://aisvg.art/blog/best-ai-svg-generators-2026), [Inkscape Tracing an Image Manual](https://inkscape-manuals.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tracing-an-image.html), [What is Recraft V4 Vector — MindStudio](https://www.mindstudio.ai/blog/what-is-recraft-v4-vector-generate-svg-logos-icons-ai), [Replicate Recraft V3 SVG API](https://replicate.com/recraft-ai/recraft-v3-svg)
